mnemonics: ACID-SIR: A Consistent, Isolated, Durable Self-Contained, Insulated, Robust database.
keyPoints: Self-Describing Nature (contains catalog with its own structure)
Insulation Between Programs and Data (program-data independence)
Data Abstraction (uses models to hide complexity)
Support of Multiple Views (different user perspectives)
Sharing of Data and Multiuser Transaction Processing (concurrent access)
commonMistakes: Confusing logical data independence (change conceptual schema without affecting application programs) with physical data independence (change internal schema without affecting conceptual schema)
Assuming that data redundancy is entirely eliminated in databases (it is controlled, not always eliminated)
Overlooking that a DBMS requires a significant upfront investment in software, hardware, and training
